It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.

Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'RIGHT': 'Moves avatar +3 cols right, charcoal moves -3 cols (opposite) (VERIFIED steps 1-4,56)', 'LEFT': 'Moves avatar -3 cols left, charcoal moves +3 cols (opposite) (VERIFIED steps 5-6,58-61)', 'DOWN': 'Moves avatar +3 rows down, charcoal moves -3 rows (opposite) (VERIFIED steps 7-10)', 'UP': 'Moves avatar -3 rows up, charcoal moves +3 rows (opposite) (VERIFIED steps 11-13)', 'SPACE': 'Moves avatar UP 3 rows only in specific conditions (step 51), no effect otherwise (steps 46,53,55)', 'MOUSE': 'No effect (VERIFIED step 43)', 'ACTION7': 'No effect (VERIFIED step 42)'}
- Object taxonomy: {'black_with_dots': 'Player avatar - black shape with 5 white dots, moves with arrow keys by 3 cells', 'charcoal_shape': 'Charcoal object that moves OPPOSITE to avatar by 3 cells', 'yellow_shape': 'Target - yellow L-shape, moves opposite to avatar, grows when avatar approaches (18<->27 pixels)', 'sky_blue_strip': 'Vertical divider at cols 30-32', 'right_border_B': 'Black strip at top-right, can merge with avatar when RIGHT pressed near border', 'yellow_border_strip': 'Yellow strip at col 63, shrinks over time (19->11 pixels)'}
- HUD map: {'bottom_row': 'Black strip at row 63', 'right_col': 'Yellow strip at col 63 - shrinking (possibly timer or progress)'}
Current level:
- World model: Avatar (42,42) after 3 LEFTs, Target moved opposite, Charcoal moved +9 cols right. Sky blue divider at cols 30-32. RIGHT caused avatar-border merge (reversible with LEFT).
- Goal model: Unknown - testing if crossing sky blue divider triggers completion
- Recent findings: ['Charcoal moves OPPOSITE to avatar (not with it) - VERIFIED step 56', 'RIGHT near border merges avatar with top-right border - VERIFIED step 56', 'LEFT reverses the merge - VERIFIED step 58', 'Target oscillates 18<->27 pixels based on avatar distance', 'Yellow border strip shrinks over time']
- Open questions: ['What happens when avatar crosses the sky blue divider?', 'What completes the level?', 'Is the yellow border a timer counting down?']
- Plan: Continue moving LEFT to cross the sky blue divider and observe what happens
- Strategy log: ['tried overlapping target: impossible since it moves opposite (step 52)', "tried SPACE multiple times: only moves avatar UP, doesn't complete level (steps 46,51,53,55)", 'tried RIGHT merge: avatar merged with border, target grew 27->45, reversible with LEFT (steps 56-58)']
- Failed probes: ['ACTION7: no effect (step 42)', 'MOUSE on target: no effect (step 43)', 'SPACE when target not adjacent: no effect (step 46)', 'UP when target above: blocked (step 50)', 'Second RIGHT after merge: no effect (step 57)']
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
I can see from the image that the avatar has crossed the sky blue divider! The avatar (black with white dots) is now on the left side of the divider, and the target (yellow L) is on the right side. The charcoal shape is also on the left side, near the divider.
Let me check the exact positions to understand what happened when crossing the divider. The level is still not complete, so crossing the divider alone isn't the win condition. Let me check the current segmentation.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
print("After crossing divider:")
for n in seg:
    if n['pixels'] > 10:
        b = n['boundary']
        r, c = min([p[0] for p in b]), min([p[1] for p in b])
        print(f"  {n['color']}: top=({r},{c}), pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Avatar crossed to left side of divider! Level not complete.
# Need to figure out what else is needed.
# Let me try moving the avatar to overlap with the charcoal shape
# Charcoal is at col ~21, avatar at col ~30
# Try LEFT to move toward charcoal
action(['LEFT', 'LEFT'])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"RIGHT": "Moves avatar +3 cols right, charcoal moves -3 (opposite)", "LEFT": "Moves avatar -3 cols left, charcoal moves +3 (opposite)", "DOWN": "Moves avatar +3 rows down, charcoal moves -3 (opposite)", "UP": "Moves avatar -3 rows up, charcoal moves +3 (opposite)", "SPACE": "Moves avatar UP 3 rows only in specific conditions", "MOUSE": "No effect", "ACTION7": "No effect"}, "object_taxonomy": {"black_with_dots": "Player avatar", "charcoal_shape": "Moves OPPOSITE to avatar", "yellow_shape": "Target - moves opposite to avatar", "sky_blue_strip": "Vertical divider at cols 30-32"}, "hud_map": {"right_col": "Yellow strip - shrinking"}, "win_pattern": {}, "level_log": {}, "cross_level_notes": ""}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Avatar crossed divider to left side. Target stayed on right. Charcoal on left near divider.", "goal_model": "Maybe need to overlap avatar with charcoal? Or reach a corner?", "recent_findings": ["Crossing divider is possible but doesn't complete level"], "open_questions": ["What completes the level?"], "current_plan": "Try overlapping avatar with charcoal shape", "strategy_log": ["tried crossing divider: no completion"], "failed_probes": ["ACTION7", "MOUSE", "SPACE", "RIGHT merge"]}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
